payload.diagram_title: Most engineers believe large language models like Qwen
quality_notes: Make the post explicitly cover observability signals like prompts, retrieval, tool calls, cost, quality, and alerts.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
Most engineers believe large language models like Qwen3.6-35B-A3B can be easily integrated into existing workflows 🤖.

However, I was wrong to think that fine-tuning and deployment would be straightforward - it took me years to learn that avoiding common pitfalls is key 💡.

When integrating these models, it's essential to consider the inputs, such as chips, that feed into the system 📈.

The retrieval and tools columns also play a critical role in shaping the model's performance.

Inputs: chips
Retrieval + Tools: columns
Runtime: Latency · Throughput · Errors · Token Spend · Escalation

Finally, monitoring runtime metrics like latency and errors is crucial to ensure the model is performing well 🚨.

So, what's your approach to integrating large language models into your workflow? 💬 Where do you focus your efforts: inputs, retrieval, or runtime? #SystemDesign #DistributedSystems #ScaleEngineering #SystemDesign #ScaleEngineering
